title: Drive Sync
subtitle: Drive-to-folder synchronization tool with drive detection and safe sync handling
description: A file synchronization tool focused on syncing known drives with selected local folders.
It uses drive identification, preview mode, safe conflict handling, and archive-safe deletes to reduce accidental data loss during drive-based workflows.
features: Detect connected drives and match them using stable drive identifiers
Create sync pairs between one known drive and one local folder path
Preview planned sync actions before applying changes
Synchronize files between configured drive and folder pairs
Preserve conflicts instead of automatically overwriting user changes
Archive deleted files instead of permanently removing them by default
Use configurable filters to skip OS-managed folders and internal sync data
Support monitor mode for connected-drive workflows
Ability to report errors encountered during use
Ability to submit suggestions and improvement ideas
statusDetails: Complete: Conflict and delete safety handling

downloadLinks.title: Product Listing
downloadLinks.body: Drive-to-folder sync utility designed for safer USB, external-drive, and cross-device workflows.

howItWorks: Drive Sync detects connected drives and identifies them using drive IDs rather than relying only on drive letters.
Configured sync pairs connect a known drive with a selected local folder path.
The sync process uses DirSync to compare files, preview changes, apply updates, preserve conflicts, and archive deleted files.
setupInstall: Create a sync pair between the drive and a local folder
apiStructure: DriveDetect handles drive scanning and drive ID resolution.
DriveSync manages known drives, sync pairs, settings, and event triggers.
DirSync performs the actual directory synchronization, conflict handling, archive handling, and filtering.

progressNotes: Drive Sync Planning (2026-07-06)
Defined the need for a drive-based sync app using drive IDs and directory sync pairs
Planned backend-first design with DriveDetect, DriveSync, and DirSync components
Drive Detection Integration (2026-07-06)
Added drive ID based matching
Designed support for added and removed drive events
Connected drive detection to external-loop and monitor workflows
Drive Sync Backend Development (2026-07-06)
Built DriveSync as the bridge between DriveDetect and DirSync
Added known drive records and sync pair handling
Added preview and sync execution support
Drive Sync App Development (2026-07-06)
Built a dark-themed desktop UI around the DriveSync backend
Added dashboard, drives, sync pairs, settings, and info sections
Connected UI controls to backend sync operations
Safety and Filter Updates (2026-07-06)
Added configurable filtering for OS-managed folders, internal sync folders, and conflict/archive data
Improved conflict handling and delete archive behavior
Added saved monitor toggle behavior
Branding and Build Preparation (2026-07-07)
Added app branding hooks and icon support
Added app info, version display, suggestion, and error-reporting controls
Prepared build logic for packaged application use

commonQuestions.question: Does Drive Sync rely on drive letters?
commonQuestions.answer: No. Drive Sync uses drive identification logic so configured drives can be recognized more reliably than by drive letter alone.

commonQuestions.question: Does Drive Sync permanently delete files?
commonQuestions.answer: By default, deleted files are archived so they can be reviewed or recovered later.

commonQuestions.question: What is Preview mode?
commonQuestions.answer: Preview mode shows planned sync actions without modifying files.
